text: Large language models (LLMs) are a type of artificial intelligence that can understand and generate human-like text. They work by learning patterns from enormous amounts of written material, known as training data. This data comes from books, articles, websites, and other texts. To use an LLM, you give it a prompt, which is a short instruction or question. This is called prompt engineering—the way you phrase your request can significantly affect the response. LLMs have impressive capabilities, such as writing stories, answering questions, and translating languages. However, they also have limits; they can produce incorrect information, known as hallucinations, and they don't truly understand meaning like humans do.
